commit.message:
security: add explicit null byte rejection to PathJail.Validate

PathJail.Validate relied on Go 1.21+ syscall-layer null byte rejection,
which made the test non-deterministic. Add explicit strings.ContainsRune
check for null bytes before any filesystem operation (CWE-158).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: cross-platform path traversal detection and WSL test resilience

Security: replace filepath.ToSlash with strings.ReplaceAll for backslash
normalization in containsDotDot() and IsSensitivePath(). filepath.ToSlash
is a no-op on Linux (backslash is not os.PathSeparator), allowing
Windows-style traversal paths like ..\etc\passwd to bypass detection.

WSL: add timeout and WSL_SKIP escape hatch to mage testwsl target.
cmdOutputTimeout helper prevents hangs when WSL is unresponsive.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: privacy-first HMAC username hashing

- Add migration 034: username_hash BYTEA column + unique index
- construct-crypto: hash_username() via HMAC-SHA256 (trim+lowercase normalization)
- construct-config: USERNAME_HMAC_SECRET env var in SecurityConfig
- construct-db: User struct gets username_hash field; all DB functions
  updated — create_user_passwordless, create_user_with_first_device,
  update_user_username now take Option<&[u8]> hash instead of plaintext;
  get_user_by_username_hash() added as primary lookup
- construct-user-service: compute hash server-side before all DB calls,
  check_username_availability uses hash lookup, plaintext never stored
- construct-auth-service: devices.rs uses hash for registration + collision check
- user-service binary: update_user_profile uses hash for collision check + update
- auth-service: recovery.rs lookup by UUID or username_hash with legacy plaintext fallback
- Fix auth_tests.rs and queue_test.rs missing username_hmac_secret field
- Update .sqlx offline query cache

Plaintext username column kept for now (migration 035 will drop it after
one-time backfill). At DB leak only HMAC hashes are exposed — irreversible
without server_secret.

commit.message:
feat: add raw timings, diffs and save metrics (#2333)

* feat: add raw timings, diffs and save metrics

Record and format build durations in Builder (src/Builder.php): compute step and total durations, output human-readable strings (ms or s) and store a duration_raw value in ms for comparisons. In Build command (src/Command/Build.php) load previous metrics from tmp/metrics.json, compute per-step diffs, annotate durations with colored +/- deltas when significant, render the metrics table with prepared rows, and persist the current metrics for the next run. This improves visibility into build timings and enables run-to-run comparisons.

* Update src/Command/Build.php

commit.message:
feat(collab): crdt-safe Y.Map element storage

- Replace Y.Array('elements') with Y.Map('elementsMap') + Y.Array('elementOrder')
  for keyed, CRDT-safe element storage (no duplicate-on-concurrent-delete/insert)
- Shallow-clone elements on Y.Map write ({ ...el }) to snapshot versionNonce
  at write time; prevents Excalidraw's in-place mutations from making stored
  references always match the latest state (causing 0 changes detected)
- Shallow-clone elements on Y.Map read for updateScene; prevents Excalidraw
  from holding Y.Map's stored objects and mutating them during move/resize,
  which was causing move operations to never emit changes to peers
- Observer origin guard: only fire updateScene for 'remote'/'init' transactions,
  not local ones, to prevent handleChange → observer → handleChange loops
- fingerprint (id:versionNonce) guard in handleChange to skip no-op onChange
  calls (e.g. appState-only changes) and prevent re-emitting remote state
- Server-side lazy migration: getOrCreate detects old Y.Array('elements')
  snapshots and migrates them to Y.Map/Y.Array on first load

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(stream): race condition in pub/sub + migration for missing index

## pub/sub race condition (slow message delivery after reconnect)

spawn_inbox_wakeup() runs in a background task and takes ~50 ms to
complete TCP-connect + SUBSCRIBE. If a message arrived during that
window, its PUBLISH was missed, and the next delivery was at the
fallback poll — previously 30 s.

Fixes:
1. Synthetic wakeup sent immediately after SUBSCRIBE completes.
   This triggers an extra poll_messages() that catches any messages
   dispatched during the subscribe race window. Also fires on every
   pub/sub reconnect (desirable — catches messages missed during drop).

2. Fallback poll interval: 30 s → 5 s.
   Caps worst-case delivery delay (concurrent reconnects, Redis blip)
   without flooding the shared queue mutex.

## migration 033: missing index on user_blocks(blocked_user_id)

Migration 019 created the user_blocks composite PK
(blocker_user_id, blocked_user_id). Only the left column is indexed.
Queries like 'who has blocked me?' (is_blocked_by in shared/db.rs)
filter by blocked_user_id and caused full sequential scans.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(messaging): batch Redis ops into single lock + add timing spans

dispatch_envelope (Kafka-disabled path) previously acquired the queue
mutex THREE times sequentially: dedup-check, stream-write, sender-store.
Between each lock/unlock tokio could yield to poll_messages, causing
unnecessary contention and context-switch overhead on every send.

Changes:
- Kafka-disabled path: single mutex acquisition for all 3 Redis ops
  (dedup mark → XADD+PUBLISH → store_sender), released once at the end
- Kafka fallback path: stream-write + sender-store combined in one lock
- send_message RPC: log dispatch_ms after dispatch_envelope returns
- dispatch_envelope: log wait_ms (time to acquire lock) + redis_ms (batch)
- poll_messages: log lock_wait_ms + xread_ms when either exceeds 20 ms
- fix(sentinel): clippy dead_code, needless_question_mark, unnecessary_cast

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(collab): fix multi-user canvas sync via Y.Map + relay buffering

Root cause: Y.Array delete-all + insert-all pattern is not CRDT-safe.
Concurrent edits from two users produced duplicate elements (4 instead of 2).

Phase 1 — fix the bug:
- Switch from Y.Array('elements') to Y.Map('elementsMap') + Y.Array('elementOrder')
- handleChange: element-level diff by version+versionNonce (only set/delete changed elements)
- Observer: reconstruct ordered array from both structures with dedup guard
- Synchronous isApplyingRemoteRef reset (remove requestAnimationFrame window)
- Lazy DB migration in CollabRoomService.getOrCreate() for old Array snapshots

Phase 2 — performance:
- Client: 50ms Yjs emit throttle with Y.mergeUpdates (reduces drag emissions ~6x)
- Server: 16ms relay buffer with Y.mergeUpdates (reduces socket frames per user)
- Server: 1MB payload size guard

Result: 1 element changed out of 100 → 6,603 bytes → 91 bytes (72x reduction)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(dht): add mDNS fallback discovery for containerized environments

- Implement MDNSDiscovery for local peer discovery via mDNS protocol
- Integrate mDNS as fallback when UDP broadcast fails
- Add LANDiscovery.handleBroadcastFailure() to track consecutive failures
- Enable mDNS automatically after 3 consecutive broadcast failures
- Register callbacks with mDNS when peers are discovered
- Update AUDIT.md with mDNS discovery implementation status
- Add comprehensive test coverage for mDNS discovery operations

Improves peer discovery reliability in Docker bridge networks, Kubernetes
pods, and other containerized environments where UDP broadcast doesn't work
reliably.

Add PartitionDetector to detect and handle network partitions in the DHT:
- Monitors network health by tracking peer responses
- Detects partition state (Healthy, Warning, Partitioned, Recovering)
- Triggers automatic re-bootstrap on partition detection
- Includes comprehensive test suite with state transitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(async): implement Lamport logical clocks for message ordering

- Add LamportClock type with atomic tick/update operations for causal ordering
- Implement MessageOrdering helper with GetTimestamp/ProcessIncoming/CurrentClock
- Add SortByLamport and FilterCausallyOrdered utilities for message batches
- Extend AsyncMessage with LamportClock and SenderClockHint fields
- Integrate message ordering into AsyncManager with three public methods
- Add 254 lines of comprehensive unit and integration tests
- Update AUDIT.md: mark storage node participation item as fixed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: toxcore
repository.description: Implementation of libtoxcore in pure Go with C bindings intended to be a drop-in replacement for libtoxcore. Will implement Noise-IK.

commit.message:
feat(async): add AsyncStorageEnabled option for optional storage node participation

- Add AsyncStorageEnabled field to Options struct (default: true)
- Conditionally initialize async manager based on configuration
- Update documentation to reflect storage node opt-out capability
- Create comprehensive implementation plan for network resilience features

This feature allows users to disable storage node participation while
retaining async messaging send capability. Opt-out is via:
  options.AsyncStorageEnabled = false

The accompanying PLAN.md document outlines 11 implementation steps to
address network topology resilience (Priority 11) and scalability
patterns (Priority 12) from the ROADMAP, including gossip-based
bootstrap, mDNS discovery, partition detection, sender-key encryption,
and pre-key DHT bundles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: dedicated worker pods, in-app notifications, report alerts

Architecture:
- Worker WORKER_QUEUES env var selects which BullMQ queues to process
- Same Docker image, different config per pod — no new images needed
- Default split: sync-worker (sync,analytics,webhooks),
  notification-worker (notifications), report-worker (reports)

In-app notifications:
- New in_app_notifications DB table with unread tracking
- Notification processor creates in-app row for every notification
- GET /v1/users/:id/notifications/inbox (list + unread count)
- PATCH /v1/users/:id/notifications/inbox/read (mark read)
- Dashboard notification bell with unread badge + dropdown panel

Report completion alerts:
- Report worker enqueues notification when report status → ready
- User sees 'Weekly Report Ready' in bell + Discord/Slack/etc.

Infrastructure:
- Helm: new notificationWorker + reportWorker deployments
- docker-compose: dedicated notification-worker + report-worker services
- Existing worker deployment sets WORKER_QUEUES via values

Documentation:
- README: updated architecture diagram, worker section, notification docs
- Helm values: documented new worker sections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: explicitly close issue in merge.md after merging to feature branch

GitHub only auto-closes issues via 'Closes #N' when merging to the
default branch. When targeting a feature branch, the issue stays open
and Ralph loops indefinitely re-attempting the same issue.

Add an explicit gh issue close step between merge and unblock.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use card.card_id instead of card.id in fetch_token_data

Scrython 2.0's ById object exposes the Scryfall UUID as card_id,
not id. This caused fetch_token_data() to raise an AttributeError
which was silently caught, returning None and preventing any tokens
from being fetched and stored.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix race condition in useOtherQueueProgress causing loading indicators to never appear (#370)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve race condition in useOtherQueueProgress causing loading indicators to never show

The hook previously checked getCurrentQueueSizes() on mount, found other=0
(before any DB ops were enqueued), and immediately set initializing=false.

- Track otherHasBeenNonZero flag in dbQueue.ts
- Export hasOtherQueueBeenActive() to check if queue was ever populated
- Call notifyListeners() on first other enqueue and when other queue empties
- Hook now waits for queue to be active before considering initialization done

Co-authored-by: WakuwakuP <8376036+WakuwakuP@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: issue/PR filtering for PRD mode and standalone mode (#7)

- Add --base "$FEATURE_BRANCH" to gh pr list so only PRs targeting
  the current feature branch are visible to Ralph
- In PRD mode, scope issue listing to --label "prd/<label>"; continue
  excluding the PRD issue itself (prd label) and blocked issues
- In standalone mode, additionally exclude all issues carrying any
  prd/* label so PRD work items are invisible
- Add preflight validation: exit non-zero with a clear diagnostic when
  --label=<x> is given but no prd/<x> issues exist and feat/<x> branch
  does not exist on origin

commit.message:
feat: --label flag, FEATURE_BRANCH variable, remove permanent_issue (#6)

- Parse optional --label=<label> argument; update usage/error messages
- Derive FEATURE_LABEL=prd/<label> and FEATURE_BRANCH=feat/<label> when
  --label is given; default FEATURE_BRANCH to 'main'
- Auto-create feat/<label> on origin from origin/main if it doesn't exist
- Initialise worktree from origin/$FEATURE_BRANCH instead of origin/main
- determine_mode() syncs to origin/$FEATURE_BRANCH
- Add {{FEATURE_BRANCH}} substitution in build_prompt()
- Remove permanent_issue from project.example.toml, ralph.sh parsing,
  and build_prompt() substitution
- Update README: CLI usage section and project config notes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ralph
repository.description: Ralph — a generic, reusable AI agent loop for GitHub projects

commit.message:
feat: add match play analysis page with per-turn suggestions

Adds a new /match/<id>/analysis/ page that analyzes each of 'Your'
turns and surfaces actionable suggestions:

- Mana Efficiency: flags turns where mana was left unused with castable
  spells available
- Play Ordering: warns when a higher-CMC spell was cast before a lower-CMC
  spell in the same main phase
- Missed Plays: highlights cards held for 3+ consecutive turns when mana
  was available
- Alternate Plays: suggests cheaper alternatives when the played spell
  cost >= 3 CMC and <60% CMC options existed

New files:
- src/services/play_advisor.py — PlayAdvisor analysis engine
- stats/utils/__init__.py — new utils package
- stats/utils/zone_utils.py — shared zone inference utilities
- stats/templates/match_analysis.html — analysis page template
- tests/test_play_advisor.py — 11 unit tests (all passing)

Modified files:
- stats/views/matches.py — add match_analysis() view, use zone_utils
- stats/views/__init__.py — export match_analysis
- stats/urls.py — register /match/<id>/analysis/ URL
- stats/templates/match_detail.html — add Analyze Plays button
- stats/static/css/style.css — add analysis page styles

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mtgas
repository.description: Magic The Gathering: Arena Stats - Django app the import log data and analyzes/visualizes

commit.message:
fix: exact label matching in determine_mode() jq filter

jq's contains() does substring matching on strings, so a label like
prd/feature-branch-workflow incorrectly matched contains(["prd"]) and
was silently excluded from the work queue.

Replace all three label checks with any(. == "label") for exact equality.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

description in architecture.mdx to reflect the new shared retry utilities
package introduced in fix(netretry) #3257.
